trackedDelta: Added tournament entry esports-world-cup-2026-cs2 (EWC 2026 CS2, Paris 12–23 Aug, catalog teams Vitality/NAVI/FaZe/Falcons). Dropped expired diamond-league-london-2026 (event done 18 July). Updated karsten-warholm (DL Silesia 23 Aug now confirmed, but after EM → not added yet). Appended a 19 July note to tour-de-france-2026 (stage 15 today, no Norwegian withdrawals; Charmig eliminated is Danish).

failures: (-5) HIGH-severity chess 'dropped-in-build' anomaly in coverage-gaps.json was not explicitly dismissed at grade time — now addressed in a follow-up note (known false-positive: Sant Martí open has no catalog chess entity; build correctly drops it).

(minor, no deduction) recurring norwegian-rights golf-split SKILL update still blocked by permissions across runs — low impact since per-event streaming in events.json is already correct; delegated to self-repair/improve or owner edit.
(minor style) Wimbledon final summaries name WBD free channel 'REX' not in streaming[] (Max + Eurosport) — additive within the same WBD rights family, and both events are past.

notes: Early Sunday run (~04:00 UTC). Today's headline events — TdF stage 15 (11:10 UTC), F1 Belgian GP race (13:00 UTC), World Cup final Spain–Argentina (19:00 UTC), The Open R4, Corales R4 — had NOT happened at run time, so no results to write. Board was already accurate and current through Saturday's evening run.
Three parallel scout subagents (cycling/TdF; broad Norwegian sweep over F1/golf/tennis/athletics/football; CS2+chess) + one independent write-time fact-checker, all with real web search.

ONLY board change: added Esports World Cup 2026 – CS2 (Paris, 12–23 Aug) as an ai-research event. The CS2 scout flagged it as a genuine coverage gap — a tier-1 CS2 tournament with multiple catalog tier2 teams (Team Vitality, NAVI, FaZe Clan, Team Falcons) that was absent from the board. An independent fact-checker confirmed dates/venue/field against Liquipedia + esportsworldcup.com and corrected the qualifier phase (7–9 Aug, not 4–6 Aug). Exact match times are not yet published, so the event carries the opening day with an estimated time + an honest note, confidence medium. Free on Twitch/YouTube; no Norwegian TV rights. 100 Thieves (rain) is NOT in the EWC field (they are a BLAST Bounty wildcard), so there is no Norwegian-player angle — the catalog teams alone make it real coverage. Verified it resolves through build-events.js isCovered (esports is entity-gated; named catalog teams pass the gate).

Cycling: TdF stage 15 start 11:10 UTC (13:10 CEST) and stage 16 ITT start 11:00 UTC re-confirmed (cyclingstage + olympics.com). No new Norwegian withdrawals — the six Norwegians (Uno-X quartet + Tobias/Anders Halland Johannessen + Per Strand Hagenes) are all still in after stage 14. Caught: Uno-X lost Anthon Charmig on stage 14 (outside time limit), but he is Danish, not one of the six Norwegians — no change to the count. GC line unchanged since stage 14. Danmark Rundt (29 Jul) still has no confirmed Norwegian broadcaster (DR + FloBikes only) → streaming left empty.

F1: Belgian GP race Sun 19 July 13:00 UTC re-confirmed against formula1.com (Viaplay), unchanged (qualifying done Sat: Antonelli pole, Hamilton P6 after practice crash, Norris P13 after grid penalty).
Golf: The Open R4 — Reitan T29 (−2), Hovland missed cut; static event, untouched. Corales R4 — Ventura T27 (−9); static. No confirmed next start for Hovland or Ventura → correctly not added.
Tennis: Ruud is in the National Bank Open Montreal entry list (ATP 1000, 1–13 Aug) but it remains date-gated (draw 31 July), no Norwegian broadcaster announced.

Athletics: Warholm won DL London 18 July (46.61). DL Silesia 23 Aug is now confirmed (Diamond League + World Athletics list him) but is AFTER EM Birmingham (10–16 Aug) and outside the ~4-week horizon; Warholm is already covered on the board via EM, so Silesia is tracked but not yet added. The DL London tracked entry was dropped (expired, event done).

Chess: no confirmed Carlsen tournament before EWC (11–15 Aug Paris, re-confirmed). Sinquefield Cup 2026 (8–21 Aug, Grand Chess Tour) clashes with EWC — Carlsen is the EWC defending champion and not a GCT signatory, so he plays EWC; a 'Carlsen at Sinquefield' listing would be a trap → not added. Aryan Tari has no confirmed upcoming event.

coverage-gaps.json HIGH-severity chess anomaly ('dropped-in-build': 1 upcoming chess event in chess.json but 0 on the board) DISMISSED as a known false-positive: the source event is 'XXVI Obert Internacional Sant Martí 2026', a minor open with no catalog chess entity in the field. build-events.js isCovered correctly drops it (chess is entity-gated; research.md names this exact Sant Martí case as out of scope). The board's chess coverage (EWC chess, Carlsen) is intact. Not a real miss — the gate is working as designed.

coverage-gaps.json entity gaps DISMISSED: Uno-X (Halland-twins result, not a future event), British Open (Reitan final-round headline — The Open is on the board as a static event), Formel 1 (Hamilton practice-crash headline — Belgian GP race is on the board Sun 19 July). All three are already-covered or past-tense, no board action.
Football: Barça Joan Gamper vs Al Ahly (Wed 19 Aug) kickoff time still officially TBC → held off the board. Lyn OBOS-ligaen fixtures unchanged.
